highlight.title: GitHub Agentic Workflows in Technical Preview
highlight.summary: <cite index="14-5,14-6">Write automation in Markdown instead of YAML. Describe what you want in natural language, and the AI agent figures out how to do it.</cite> <cite index="14-24">Security-first design: Read-only by default with sandboxed execution, network isolation, SHA-pinned dependencies, and sanitized write operations through safe outputs.</cite>

resources.title: Claude Code vs Codex: Terminal AI Tool Battle
resources.why_now: <cite index="7-10,7-11">Claude Code hits 80.8% on SWE-bench. Codex uses 3x fewer tokens.</cite> <cite index="3-18,3-19,3-20">Claude Code brings Anthropic's AI directly into your terminal. Unlike web-based chatbots, it runs locally and can read, edit, and create files in your project.</cite>

resources.title: Socket Supply Chain Security Innovation
resources.why_now: <cite index="37-1,37-2">This is exactly the problem Socket was built to solve. While most tools wait for a CVE to be assigned, Socket detects malicious behavior in packages before they ever make it into a vulnerability database.</cite> <cite index="1-30">The CLI tool that you can run locally is fast enough to include in pre-commit hooks without adding noticeable delay.</cite>

resources.title: Cursor vs Windsurf: AI Editor Evolution
resources.why_now: <cite index="22-13,22-14">Windsurf's SWE-1.5 achieves near-frontier coding quality at dramatically faster inference. For developers who spend all day in an AI IDE, the speed difference is noticeable: less waiting, more flow.</cite> <cite index="21-20,21-21,21-22">Windsurf's Cascade agent is designed for multi-step autonomous tasks: it reads terminal output, iterates on errors, and chains actions. Cursor's Composer does similar work but gives you more control (and less autonomy).</cite>

resources.title: WordPress @wordpress/build Replaces Webpack
resources.why_now: <cite index="9-43,9-44">It replaces the webpack and Babel pipeline with a significantly faster build engine, auto-generates PHP registration files from package.json conventions, and handles scripts, script modules, and styles in one pass. It works through convention-based folder discovery: a packages/ directory for JavaScript packages and a routes/ directory for admin page routes, each auto-discovered with no configuration.</cite> <cite index="9-45">Gutenberg already uses it to build all of its 100+ packages.</cite>

claude_take: 2026 is the year AI moves from autocomplete to autonomous workflows—GitHub Actions now speaks Markdown instead of YAML, CLI tools like Claude Code operate with 80% SWE-bench accuracy, and even WordPress ditched webpack for convention-over-configuration builds. The tooling wars are shifting from feature parity to speed and intelligence.
